title: Listening - Part 4: The Impact of Podcasts
description: You will hear five short extracts in which people talk about their habit of listening to podcasts. You must complete two tasks while listening.

content.audio_script: Speaker 1: I started listening to history podcasts because [21]I felt like my education had been a bit narrow and I wanted to fill in the gaps[/21]. I didn't want to be the person at dinner parties who didn't know the basics. It’s been great. But the real benefit is that [26]it’s made my daily commute on the bus something I actually look forward to[/26]. It used to be dead time, but now I’m learning about the French Revolution while stuck in traffic. || Speaker 2: I’m a runner, and [22]I initially used podcasts to distract me from the boredom of long-distance training sessions[/22]. Music wasn't doing it for me anymore. Surprisingly, [27]I’ve found that I’ve become much more articulate when discussing complex topics[/27]. Hearing experts debate issues has given me a much better vocabulary and structure for expressing my own opinions at work. || Speaker 3: I’m a high-flyer in a law firm, and [23]I listen to comedy podcasts purely as a way to switch off my brain after a twelve-hour day[/23]. I need to laugh to stay sane. But [28]what I really value is the sense of intimacy you get with the hosts[/28]. You feel like you're part of a conversation between friends, and when you spend all day in a formal office, that warmth is really refreshing. || Speaker 4: I’m a teacher, and [24]I use podcasts to stay up to date with the latest developments in educational psychology[/24]. It’s much easier than reading academic journals. What I’ve gained, though, is [29]the ability to multi-task effectively[/29]. I listen while I’m doing the ironing or cooking, and it’s made me realise I can be productive and learn even when I’m doing mundane chores. || Speaker 5: I started a tech podcast of my own, but [25]I listen to other people’s shows primarily to see how they structure their narratives[/25]. I’m basically studying the competition. Interestingly, [30]it’s actually encouraged me to pick up my old hobby of creative writing again[/30]. Hearing so many diverse stories has made me realise that everyone has a unique tale to tell, and it’s given me the push I needed to start my own novel.

task1.title: Task One
task1.instruction: For questions 21 – 25, choose from the list (A – H) each speaker's main reason for listening to podcasts.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Speaker 1 wanted to 'fill in the gaps' in their education.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Speaker 2 wanted to 'distract' themselves from the boredom of running.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Speaker 3 uses comedy podcasts to 'switch off my brain' after work.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Speaker 4 uses them to 'stay up to date' with developments in their field.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Speaker 5 listens to study how others 'structure their narratives'.

options.A: to remain informed about professional trends
options.C: to help them fall asleep at night
options.D: to keep up with current affairs
options.E: to address deficiencies in their knowledge
options.G: to unwind after a demanding day
options.H: to analyse the techniques of others

task2.title: Task Two
task2.instruction: For questions 26 – 30, choose from the list (A – H) what each speaker has gained from the experience.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Speaker 1 now looks forward to their 'daily commute' which used to be dead time.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Speaker 2 has become 'much more articulate' when discussing topics.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Speaker 3 values the 'sense of intimacy' and warmth from the hosts.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Speaker 4 can now 'multi-task effectively' while doing chores.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Speaker 5 was encouraged to return to 'creative writing'.

options.B: a desire to travel more widely
options.D: renewed interest in a creative pursuit
options.F: a more positive attitude towards travel
options.G: the inspiration to start a business
